commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove -lbnetapi from Makefile
- Update README.md links

- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

commit.message:
feat(model-3d): integrate splitFaceByPolygon into Rectangle tool

When drawing a rectangle on an existing HEMesh face, the face is now
split in-place rather than creating a separate thin box object. This
enables push-pull to work naturally on the inner rectangle face.
Falls back to thin box creation for ground plane or when split fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): add splitFaceByPolygon kernel function

Implements the core polygon-split algorithm that all drawing-on-face
tools (Rectangle, Circle, Arc, Polygon) will use. For each polygon edge,
extends it to an infinite line and splits all tracked faces sequentially,
then identifies the inner face by centroid proximity.

Also fixes a critical bug in splitFaceByLine where the t parameter passed
to splitEdge was not adjusted for shared edges whose stored half-edge
direction is opposite to the face's half-edge direction. This caused
splits on box faces (or any face with shared edges) to create vertices
at the wrong positions (1-t instead of t).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TypeScript build: remove duplicate getDb import, add SanctionAction type import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enable Firestore offline persistence to fix cross-device sync

signOut() was cancelling in-flight Firestore writes causing data loss.
With persistence, set() commits to IndexedDB immediately before signOut()
can invalidate the auth token, ensuring reliable cross-device sync.

Add rate limiting and storage capacity protection

- New _middleware.js applies IP-based rate limiting (20 writes/min default)
  to all write operations under /api/ using Cloudflare KV
- New _utils.js provides shared checkRateLimit and checkStorageCapacity helpers
- Storage capacity check (default 5 GB) added to POST clip, POST upload-url,
  and PUT file upload — returns 503 with user-friendly message when full
- Gracefully degrades: rate limiting skipped if KV namespace not bound
- Configurable via RATE_LIMIT_WRITES_PER_MIN and MAX_STORAGE_BYTES env vars

Add paper trading, multi-target TP, walk-forward backtest, portfolio analytics, and grid trading

(2) multi-target take profit with partial exits at configurable levels,
(3) walk-forward backtesting for strategy robustness validation,
(4) portfolio analytics dashboard with equity curves and drawdown charts,
(5) grid trading bot with arithmetic/geometric modes and limit order management.

commit.message:
fix: WebSocket 穩定性與 Telegram callback 授權驗證

- sendRPC 前檢查 ws.readyState 避免在未連線時發送
- ws.terminate() 確保 error 事件後正確觸發重連
- callback_query 加入 TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID 授權檢查，阻擋未授權操作
- JSON.parse 加入 try/catch 防止格式錯誤導致崩潰

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ObjectDisposedException: HttpClient disposed during active stream

Root cause: ApplicationStopping handler calls llmAdapter.Dispose()
which disposed _http while an SSE streaming response was in flight.
This caused the error to appear in the chat UI.

Fix 1: LLMAdapter.Dispose() — stop disposing _http.
        _http is a process-lifetime singleton; the OS reclaims the
        socket on process exit. Disposing it mid-stream is unsafe.

Fix 2: ChatOnce() — catch ObjectDisposedException → return null
        (server shutting down; treat as silent failure).

Fix 3: StreamAsync() — flag pattern to handle ObjectDisposedException
        before the first yield (catch inside yield iterator is illegal).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Mobile/UI polish: chevron left, new-conv auto-expand, centered tabs

- Chat-peek: move ▼ arrow to left side (matches results chevron position)
- clearHistory(): auto-expand chat panel on mobile when New conversation is tapped
- Mode tabs row: change justify-content space-between → center so Search/ICP/
  Discovery and New conversation are all grouped and centered together
  (works on both desktop and mobile)

commit.message:
Fix Stripe Connect auth: JWT header + auth callback cookie propagation

Root cause: auth/callback/route.ts set session cookies via cookies() from
next/headers, but the Set-Cookie values were not included in the explicit
NextResponse.redirect() response returned to the browser. Result: browser
never received session cookies, so server-side createClient() returned null
user (Unauthorized 401) even though the browser Supabase client had a session.

Changes:
- auth/callback/route.ts: rewritten to use request/response cookie pattern
  (@supabase/ssr recommended approach) — cookies are now set directly on the
  redirect response via response.cookies.set(), ensuring the browser receives
  the session after OAuth/email confirmation
- onboarding/page.tsx: handleStripeConnect now sends getSession() access_token
  as Authorization: Bearer header instead of relying on cookies
- api/onboarding/stripe-connect: verifies JWT from Authorization header via
  admin.auth.getUser(token); falls back to cookie auth if no header present
- api/instructor/stripe-connect: same JWT header auth via shared resolveUser()
- instructor/payout-setup/page.tsx: loadStatus() and handleConnect() pass
  Authorization header so dashboard Stripe Connect also works reliably

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
